figures.caption: TABLE III. Per-particle input features for the jet flavor identification and jet energy regression tasks, following Ref. [35].

FIG. 5. Best validation loss for the jet energy regression task as a function of the number of training jets, comparing the three model variants (blue: Baseline; red: Latentaugmented; green: Linear-probe). Each curve shows the median across three random seeds, and the shaded bands indicate the interquartile range.

FIG. 8. Missing momentum performance at the largest training-set size (blue: Baseline; red: Latent-augmented; green: Linear-probe). Left, middle: Recoil resolution parallel (σ(u∥)) and perpendicular (σ(u⊥)) to the generator-level pmiss

FIG. 9. Capacity-control comparison for jet flavor identification: ROC curves at the full training set for the Baseline (blue) and Baseline-large (orange) models. Dashed lines show the light-flavor jet mis-identification rate vs b-jet identification efficiency; solid lines show the c-jet mis-identification rate.
FIG. 10. Capacity-control comparison for jet energy regression: inclusive jet-energy response distribution at the full training set for the Baseline (blue) and Baseline-large (orange) models.
